author: claude
subject: Review of RAO ingest-archive impl (prompt-rao-archive-rem): 5 High / 10 Medium / 12 Low; report to codex
summary: Reviewed the rem-side ingest-archive implementation (commits e7b9e5f..4ca69c3: archive_ingest.rs ~2000 lines + lib.rs ~1160) against prompt-rao-archive-rem.md / design-ingest-v2-rao-archive.md Part A. claude read the three security/correctness cores line-by-line (hand-rolled glob, shelled tar engine, tar-header index parser); three parallel lanes covered CLI/restore, scan/planner/clustering, test/DoD coverage; all High findings verified in code before publishing. Report: docs/code-review-rao-ingest-archive-2026-06-15.md. Gates green (fmt, clippy -D warnings, 87 cli tests pass). Verdict: all 6 scope items' happy path works and is tested incl. keyed+keyless blob single-file ranged extract; no wire-format change. HIGHS: H1 tar option-injection in create_wrapper_tar (no `--` before the member operand; member comes from the untrusted source tree incl. blob-matched dir names -> GNU tar --checkpoint-action=exec RCE; High now, Critical for third-party ingest; fix = arg('--')); H2 panic in parse_pax_records (slice data[cursor+space+1..cursor+len-1] inverts when len<space+2, e.g. crafted record '01 x\n'; reachable from a corrupt/hostile .remwrap.tar on the restore-validation path; fix = guard len>=space+2); H3 the gating A3.5 fidelity test silently early-returns in CI because CI installs libarchive-tools but not `attr` (setfattr/getfattr) -> the key DoD deliverable never runs, counts green; H4 has_xattrs shells to getfattr and returns false when absent -> silent xattr DATA LOSS (violates never-silent); H5 scan density rule lacks topmost-only + don't-climb-past-compliant-subtree, so the design's AppData-blobbed/Documents-granular example fails. MEDIUMS: dialect not pinned (prod accepts GNU tar fallback, only bsdtar fidelity-tested); .idx records lossy U+FFFD keys for non-UTF-8 members (unlocatable for restore - the files wrapping exists to preserve); round_up_512 overflow; --scan-only does full hash+wrap work (not a cheap dry run) and holds every path in RAM; straggler verdict missing + sanity-ceiling mis-modeled; record_dir_stats leaf-phantom; validate rehashes whole blob; customer manifest omits mtime (design A5); rem restore vs archive extract naming. LOWS incl. unbounded recursion depth, unchecked += byte rollups, cross-tree hardlink collapses whole archive to one blob (spec-faithful sharp edge - raise with owner), glob [^] nit, --blob-entry/--out arg nits. Untested: file-fallback wrap via pipeline, --no-index, .idx rebuild, density verdicts. CODEX TASK: work the report's suggested order (H1+H2 first, then H3+H4 CI/xattr, then H5+scan, then M4/M5 scan-only cost). Spec-side: dialect pinning (M1) and L3 hardlink edge need a decision before code.

author: claude
subject: Ingest deferred items 1/2/2b resolved; RAO 1.1 (xattr metadata preservation) pulled forward
summary: Working through the 4 deferred ingest-archive review items with owner (design/discussion). RESOLVED: item 1 (non-UTF-8 member names) = single human-readable name field with ls/cat -v reversible backslash-hex escaping (escape the backslash itself for bijection), no base64, no lossy U+FFFD; one identifier across .remwrap.idx + customer manifest + restore request; replaces from_utf8_lossy. Item 2 (cheap --scan-only) = classification-only walk (stat + decide + native_status, update rollups only; no hashing/tar/.idx/file-list), shared classifier so scan provably predicts build, xattr detection via the `xattr` syscall crate not getfattr subprocess (also removes external attr dep, supersedes the H4 fail-loud guard). Item 2b (xattr policy): xattrs no longer force wrap — junk denylist (quarantine/wherefroms/lastused/Spotlight) dropped silently; meaningful xattrs (e.g. Finder color tags) preserved on a clean native entry. That pulled RAO 1.1 FORWARD (1.0 not frozen, manifest reserves metadata_preservation_data, 1.0 readers already tolerate it, exercises the 1.x mechanism for real). RAO 1.1 scope = xattrs ONLY (mtime already in 1.0 via pax keyword w/ fractional secs; ownership deliberately excluded by 1.0 - don't reverse; mode-beyond-executable/ACLs deferred); CBOR byte strings hold raw xattr values so no base64; schema_version emits 1.1 only when non-empty (byte-identical 1.0 otherwise); small xattrs annotate, large (>~4KiB, basically only resource forks - rare in modern media) wrap. AppleDouble: rem does NOT transcode ._<->xattr (faithful byte engine); sutradhara MAY offer optional recorded staging-time ._->native-xattr normalization on the staged copy so rem uniformly sees native xattrs (rem contract unchanged either way; tooling Netatalk-style on Linux, dot_clean is mac-only). Docs: ingest-archive-deferred-items-design-v0.1.md (items 1/2/2b) + new rao-1.1-metadata-preservation-design-v0.1.md. STILL OPEN: item 3 (rem restore naming/alias - needs harness check), item 4 (cross-tree hardlink edge), and one small sub-decision (xattr denylist vs allowlist default - lean denylist). NB: SSH push still failing this session (agent dropped); commits local, gardener to push.

author: claude
subject: Ingest deferred items 3 & 4 resolved (foreign-formats-as-plugins direction; flatten hardlinks)
summary: Closed items 3 and 4 of the ingest-archive deferred set. Item 3 (rem restore naming): owner reframed - BRU should not be in rem core; native (RAO, rem writes) vs foreign/legacy (BRU, old tar; read-only, reverse-engineered, per-deployment) are different categories; foreign formats become PLUGINS (BRU = its own project implementing rem's published foreign-format-driver trait from format-driver-streaming-boundary.md; rem core ships zero foreign formats; binary assembled compile-time from core + chosen plugins, NOT dynamic loading). This dissolves the naming collision at the root: BRU-specific 'archive restore' leaves core, so 'rem restore'/'archive extract' cleanly owns native RAO restore and foreign restore is the generic 'rem archive <op> --format <plugin>' dispatch, plugin-gated. Its own architecture work item (promote driver trait to published extension point; move remanence-bru out of core workspace; pairs with tape-platform-seam-design) - own design doc to follow. Item 4 (cross-tree hardlink edge): the whole-input-collapse is self-inflicted by preserving hardlinks via wrapping the common-ancestor dir (cross-subtree links -> common ancestor is root -> whole archive blobbed). DECISION: flatten hardlinks - each name becomes an ordinary native RAO entry with its own bytes; nlink>1 no longer a wrap trigger. No data lost (every byte archived; only the inode link, a source-FS property, is dropped); 'same content' already captured by per-file sha256 + catalog asset keying; recorded never-silent. Tradeoff: bytes stored once per name (negligible for rare media hardlinks; future sha256 bundle-dedup or a real RAO hardlink entry-type if ever needed - not the dir-blob hack). Removes collect_hardlink_roots + its extra tree walk, common-ancestor logic, collapse edge, climb-capping band-aids; native_status drops the hardlink branch - net simplification. REMAINING OPEN: only the small xattr denylist-vs-allowlist default (lean denylist). Docs updated: ingest-archive-deferred-items-design-v0.1.md (all 4 items now resolved). Push still failing this session (SSH agent dropped); commits local; gardener to push.

author: claude
subject: Item 4 finalized: native hardlinks + entry-type scope principle + sparse-via-upstream-compression
summary: Item 4 evolved well past the original hardlink edge. FINAL decisions (supersede the interim 'flatten' lean): (1) HARDLINKS handled NATIVELY like tar - typeflag 1, zero payload, target=primary entry's in-object path in linkname/pax linkpath, manifest entry_type=hardlink+link_target (reuses symlink machinery); first occurrence stores bytes, later names are link entries. Chosen over flatten because it completes the file-tree entry set, dissolves the cross-tree-collapse edge (tar hardlinks are in-archive path refs, no common-ancestor concept), stores bytes once, preserves the link, stock-tar-faithful, pre-freeze, consistent with the symlink/dir native-typeflag decision. Delta over symlinks (modest): referential integrity (target must resolve to a real primary in-object), deterministic primary selection (first non-excluded), inode-group detection, restore ordering + PFR-resolves-to-primary. Edges (group split across blob boundary, or primary excluded) fall back to independent copy. Supersedes rao-nonregular-entries' hardlink deferral - hardlinks now IN scope. (2) ENTRY-TYPE SCOPE PRINCIPLE (answer to 'why not full tar'): RAO native set = {regular, symlink, dir, hardlink} = 'a faithful tree of files'; boundary is content-vs-OS-kernel-handle. Excluded on principle + safety: char/block devices, FIFOs, sockets (zero content, kernel handles, device-node/setuid extraction hazard - RAO already drops ownership/setuid). The constrained subset is the feature (determinism, hostile-input safety, re-implementable-from-short-spec); full tar would forfeit those. Non-content types -> skip-and-record or blob-the-subtree. TO FOLD INTO published spec rationale (the why-not-X FAQ). (3) SPARSE / VM IMAGES: rejected a RAO sparse profile (would forfeit stock-tar extractability, changes body layout so needs a hard detect-and-refuse gate, pollutes the media spec; and tar's sparse formats are a vendor minefield - no POSIX standard, GNU 0.0/0.1/1.0+oldgnu+star incompatible, FS-dependent nondeterministic hole maps). INSTEAD: large-image efficiency = optional selective per-artifactclass UPSTREAM COMPRESSION in sutradhara (compress-before-archive, same pattern as AppleDouble merge; zstd crushes holes AND compresses real data, beats elision; rem archives a normal dense file so RAO stays pure). Conditions: selective by policy (never media), pin+record compressor/level (byte-stable fanout; compress before encrypt), record original logical sha256 + verify-after-decompress, sutradhara owns symmetric decompress. PFR dies for compressed objects but VM images restore whole. Boundary: partial-access-into-image -> seekable compression or elision later. Doc: ingest-archive-deferred-items-design-v0.1.md item 4 rewritten + 2 new sections. REMAINING: only the xattr denylist-vs-allowlist micro-decision (the last point). Push still parked (SSH agent).

author: claude
subject: Last point closed: xattr denylist/allowlist is ruleset-configurable with a fail-safe default — entire deferred-items thread now decision-complete
summary: Final micro-decision (owner's idea, better than my 'lean denylist'): xattr mode is configured IN THE RULESET, not a single global choice. Model: (1) universal junk baseline (macOS ephemerals: quarantine/wherefroms/lastused/Spotlight/FinderInfo noise) always dropped, built into rem, updated as OSes evolve - rulesets never re-type it; (2) per-ruleset stance via option-style directives (name-based, ruleset-global, NOT per-path): 'option xattr-mode denylist' (keep all except baseline + xattr-drop additions) or 'option xattr-mode allowlist' (keep only xattr-keep entries); (3) absent any directive -> fail-safe default = denylist stance + baseline only. Configurability strengthens never-silent (safe default protects the unaware; allowlist is an explicit recorded per-source choice; all drops counted). Fits the ruleset-as-policy home alongside blob/exclude/expect/case-insensitive, and the rem/sutradhara split (rem parses+applies; sutradhara names the ruleset). Doc updated (ingest-archive-deferred-items-design-v0.1.md xattr section). THREAD NOW DECISION-COMPLETE: items 1,2,2b,3(direction),4 all resolved. Remaining is execution only: (a) codex impl of items 1/2/4/xattr-policy + RAO 1.1 spec edit + format work (design doc rao-1.1-metadata-preservation-design-v0.1.md written); (b) two write-ups owed - the foreign-format-plugin architecture design doc (item 3 spun-off), and folding the entry-type scope principle (content-not-kernel-handles) into the published RAO spec rationale. All commits local this session; SSH agent dropped so push pending (gardener to carry, or manual push when key loaded).

author: claude
subject: Wrote codex-ready design docs (rem) + sutra prompt for all ingest-v2 follow-up decisions
summary: Turned the session's decisions into implementation-ready artifacts. REM design docs (codex work orders): (1) rao-hardlinks-design-v0.1.md - native hardlinks (ustar typeflag 1, manifest entry_type=hardlink+link_target carrying the primary's content coords, referential integrity, deterministic primary selection, restore via link(2), edges) + the entry-type scope principle (content vs kernel handles: regular/symlink/dir/hardlink IN; devices/FIFOs/sockets OUT on principle+safety; to fold into the published spec rationale); supersedes the nonregular-entries hardlink deferral. (2) ingest-layer-implementation-design-v0.1.md - 4 units: A member-name reversible \xHH escaping (replaces from_utf8_lossy), B cheap classification-only --scan-only (shared classifier so scan predicts build; xattr via the `xattr` syscall crate, drops the attr/getfattr dependency), C xattr ingest policy (universal junk baseline always dropped; ruleset option xattr-mode denylist|allowlist + xattr-keep/drop; fail-safe default; small->1.1 annotation/large->wrap; never-silent), D hardlink ingest (detect by dev,ino; emit native primary+links; remove collect_hardlink_roots/common-ancestor machinery). (3) foreign-format-plugin-architecture-design-v0.1.md - foreign/legacy formats (BRU) become compile-time plugins out of core; published foreign-format-driver trait (promote the format-driver-streaming-boundary sketch); generic --format dispatch plugin-gated; native restore verb; incremental sequencing; CI guard that core has no foreign-format dep. RAO 1.1 doc already written (rao-1.1-metadata-preservation) - updated its xattr-policy section to the resolved ruleset-configurable model. SUTRA: prompt-ingest-v2-sutra-followups.md (self-contained prompt for the sutra Claude instance) - AppleDouble ._->xattr staging merge, upstream compression for sparse/VM images (not a RAO sparse profile), shared member-name escaping contract, ruleset xattr directives + restore-verb alignment; encapsulates decisions AND reasoning. INDEX updated; nonregular-entries doc marked superseded re hardlinks. All commits local (SSH agent dropped this session; push pending). Nothing implemented by me - design/handoff only per devloop role.

author: claude
subject: Wrote specs/rao-1.1-specification.md (additive minor: hardlinks + xattr preservation + entry-scope), 1.0 untouched
summary: Per owner: I do the RAO spec updates now, as a SEPARATE 1.1 spec doc, keeping the published 1.0 untouched. Read the current 1.0 spec sections first to stay consistent with how symlinks/dirs were specified (typeflag 2/5, entry_type/link_target optional manifest keys, linkpath, reserved metadata_preservation_data empty-in-1.0 + readers tolerate non-empty, reader gates on MAJOR version, §10 additive-minor rule). Wrote specs/rao-1.1-specification.md as a normative additive delta on 1.0: (1) version gating - REMANENCE.schema_version=1.1 only when a 1.1 feature is used (else byte-identical 1.0); manifest CBOR schema_version integer stays 1 (reserved-container/optional-key extension surface); 1.0-reader behavior both fail-safe (ignores xattr metadata = reads files fine; rejects typeflag 1 with UnsupportedTarTypeflag = fail-closed, not misinterpretation) -> why it's a minor not a new format_id, consistent with 1.0 §10. (2) Entry-type scope statement (normative): {regular,symlink,dir,hardlink} in; char/block devices, FIFOs, sockets OUT on principle+safety (readers MUST reject typeflags 3/4/6). (3) Native hardlinks (typeflag 1): zero payload, in-object target in linkname/linkpath, manifest entry_type=hardlink + link_target + CARRY THE PRIMARY'S content coords, referential integrity (target must resolve to a regular-file primary appearing BEFORE the link; new error InvalidHardlinkTarget), deterministic primary selection, restore primary-before-links via link(2) + traversal safety, stock-tar recreates hardlinks. (4) xattr preservation: metadata_preservation_data.xattrs map, CBOR byte-string values (no base64), deterministic encoding, empty when none (byte-stability), policy is ingest-side, large xattr -> wrap. (5) errors + vectors + conformance. FLAGGED a reviewable structural alternative IN the doc: hardlinks could instead fold into 1.0 (symmetric entry set) leaving 1.1 = xattrs only; chose 1.0-stability per owner's framing, easy to flip. The two design docs (rao-hardlinks, rao-1.1-metadata-preservation) are the rationale/work-orders behind this spec. INDEX updated. Design-only per devloop role - codex implements to this spec.

author: claude
subject: Moved hardlinks into RAO 1.0 spec (symmetric entry set); 1.1 trimmed to xattrs-only
summary: Per owner: move hardlinks from the 1.1 spec into 1.0 (the symmetric entry-set alternative I had flagged). Edited specs/rao-1.0-specification.md to add native hardlinks (typeflag 1) in parallel to the existing symlinks/dirs, reading each section's current text first for exact matches: typeflag table §4.3.4 (added `1` (0x31) Hardlink; reworded the reject sentence to 'entry set = regular/hardlink/symlink/dir, excludes device/FIFO/socket'); header table mode + typeflag + linkname rows; pax linkpath keyword + reader fallback (now symlink+hardlink); entry frame §4.6.1 (typeflag 0/1/2/5; hardlink size=0; new **Hardlinks** paragraph: in-object target, referential integrity = target must resolve to a regular-file primary appearing BEFORE the link, deterministic primary selection); §4.6.5 no-payload-hash (hardlink shares primary's hash); manifest §4.7.2 (entry_type adds hardlink; size_bytes/chunk_count/file_sha256/first_chunk_lba 'a hardlink carries its primary's'; link_target for hardlinks = primary in-object path); reader dispatch §4.9 (typeflag 1 → verify target resolves to a delivered primary, InvalidHardlinkTarget otherwise); §1.5 scope (faithful tree of files: regular/hardlink/symlink/dir IN; devices/FIFO/socket excluded on principle; ownership not preserved; xattrs are 1.x); §11.1 errors (+InvalidHardlinkTarget); restore traversal (materialize primary before link, link(2)); §13 vectors (hardlink round-trip to shared inode + negative). Rewrote specs/rao-1.1-specification.md down to a clean xattrs-ONLY minor: now purely additive/fully-backward-compatible (1.0 reader reads a 1.1 object correctly, just ignoring xattrs — no more typeflag-1-rejection wrinkle since hardlinks are 1.0). Coherence verified by grep (28 hardlink refs in 1.0; 1.1 mentions hardlinks only in the 'they're in 1.0' pointer). INDEX 1.1 row updated. The rao-hardlinks design doc already targeted 1.0 for its spec edits, so it stays aligned; the rao-1.1-metadata-preservation design doc remains the rationale behind the trimmed 1.1 spec. Design-only per devloop role.

author: claude
subject: Applied GPT spec-review fixes to RAO 1.0/1.1 (hardlink model made consistent; vector/ref/stale-text fixes)
summary: GPT reviewed my hardlinks-into-1.0 edits; per owner, ignored finding #1 (reference impl doesn't match — tracked in the codex work orders) and fixed the six spec-internal findings, all verified as real bugs I introduced. #2 (HIGH, the substantive one): hardlink size/hash/coordinate semantics were internally inconsistent (tar size=0 + 'no payload' vs manifest 'carries the primary's size_bytes/chunk_count/file_sha256/first_chunk_lba' vs file_sha256 'regular-only'). Rather than GPT's suggested split-the-fields patch, chose the tar-faithful model: a hardlink entry carries NO content fields of its own (size_bytes 0, chunk_count 0, first_chunk_lba null, no file_sha256 — exactly like a symlink), and a hardlinked name's content/hash/PFR resolve through link_target to the primary. Removes the inconsistency by construction (no cross-check carve-outs); reverted the 'carry the primary's coords' manifest rows, the no-payload-hash line, and the reader-dispatch line accordingly. #3 (MED): moved the positive 'hardlink primary+link → shared inode' round-trip out of the negative-vector list into §13.1 positives (and added symlink + empty-dir positives, which weren't enumerated); kept absent/non-regular-target as the negative. #4 (MED): RAO 1.1 §2.1 referenced an 'ingest member-name escaping' that isn't a normative RAO primitive — declared non-UTF-8 xattr NAMES out of scope (drop-and-record or wrap) instead, removing the dangling ref (names are ASCII by namespace convention anyway). #5 (LOW): INDEX 1.1 row now points hardlinks→rao-hardlinks-design, xattrs→rao-1.1-metadata-preservation. #6 (LOW): rao-1.1-metadata-preservation design doc no longer lists hardlinks as a can't-be-native wrap case. #7 (LOW): §10 'future hardlink identity metadata' landing-zone phrase reworded (hardlinks are native 1.0 now). Coherence grepped clean. NOTE: the rao-hardlinks-design doc and ingest-layer-impl doc still describe option-A 'carry the primary's coords' — need a follow-up edit to align them to the resolve-via-link_target model before codex implements (flagged to owner).

author: claude
subject: Aligned hardlink design docs + decision record to the option-B (resolve-via-link_target) model
summary: After switching the RAO 1.0 spec to the tar-faithful hardlink model (option B: hardlink entry carries no content fields; content/hash/PFR resolve via link_target to the primary), aligned the three docs that still described option A ('carry the primary's coords'): rao-hardlinks-design-v0.1.md (manifest §3.2, spec-edit checklist §7, vectors §8, impl pointers §9), ingest-layer-implementation-design-v0.1.md (unit D emit step), and ingest-archive-deferred-items-design-v0.1.md (item-4 delta list). Final grep across specs/ and docs/ confirms no residual coord-carrying language. Spec + all work orders + decision record now consistent on the one hardlink model. Codex can implement from a coherent set.

author: claude
subject: GPT round-3 fixes: propagated the option-B hardlink model into PFR, verifier, trust diagrams, and 3 doc lines
summary: GPT round-3 found six residual spots where the spec/docs still assumed every entry has direct content fields (consequences of the option-B hardlink model I'd only propagated to the manifest/reader). All real; all fixed. #1 (HIGH): RAO 1.0 §6 PFR now has an explicit Hardlinks rule — a hardlinked name resolves link_target to the primary and uses the PRIMARY's first_chunk_lba/size_bytes; MUST NOT treat it as empty/invalid. #2 (MED): §7.4 verifier profile now distinguishes regular entries (match size_bytes/file_sha256/first_chunk_lba/chunk_count) from hardlinks (match link_target, zero/null content fields, valid primary) and symlink/dir (zero/null). #3 (MED): rao-hardlinks-design §5 reader/restore + verifier lines de-coord-carried (was 'target + shared content coordinates' / 'link's shared file_sha256'). #4 (MED): rao-1.1-metadata-preservation design doc xattr-name line aligned to the spec (non-UTF-8 names out of scope, not item-1 escaping). #5 (LOW): INDEX deferred-items row updated pending→current/all-resolved. #6 (LOW): both chain-of-trust diagrams (§4.7.3, §7.1) qualified — content fields are direct for regular entries; a hardlink resolves via link_target to its primary. Final grep clean. The option-B model is now consistent across the entire spec (manifest, reader, PFR, verifier, diagrams, vectors, errors) and all design docs.
